label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's break down the sequence of actions and see if the person ends up back at the starting point. 1. Take 9 steps forward. 2. Turn around. Now the person is facing the opposite direction. 3. Take 9 steps. Since they turned around, these are 9 steps backward in relation to the original direction. 4. Turn around again. Now the person is facing the original direction again. After taking 9 steps forward and then 9 steps backward (relative to the original direction), the person would have ended up back at the starting point.
